rows.title: Greece's National Cyber Security Authority to participate in Cyber Europe 2026 exercise targeting transport infrastructure resilience
rows.summary:
Greece's National Cyber Security Authority is participating in the design and delivery of Cyber Europe 2026, a two-day pan-European cyber crisis exercise scheduled for June 2026, focused on transport sector critical infrastructure under the auspices of the European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA).

On 27 March 2026, the National Cyber Security Authority of Greece published an announcement confirming its active role in the national planning and implementation of Cyber Europe 2026. The authority states that the exercise carries no financial penalty element and involves no finding of breach; it is a cooperative preparedness exercise organised by ENISA in collaboration with national cybersecurity authorities and relevant European bodies across EU member states.

The National Cyber Security Authority states that the exercise is designed to strengthen the collective capacity of EU member states to prevent, detect and respond to complex cyber threats. The authority notes that the two-day exercise will centre on cyber crisis scenarios affecting the transport sector, with particular emphasis on critical infrastructure such as ports and railway networks. The authority further states that participants will have the opportunity to develop their skills and prepare effectively for scenarios reflecting the continuously evolving cybersecurity landscape.

The National Cyber Security Authority states that its participation confirms its commitment to reinforcing European cyber resilience. The authority notes that through its involvement it contributes to the development and implementation of strategies intended to ensure the protection of critical infrastructure in the transport sector and to strengthen overall security at both national and European Union level. The authority presents the exercise as a platform for cooperation among participating entities across the EU member state community.
